title: Free Achilles tendon strain during selected rehabilitation, locomotor, jumping, and landing tasks.
authors: Devaprakash D|Graham DF|Barrett RS|Lloyd DG|Obst SJ|Kennedy B|Adams KL|Kiely RJ|Hunter A|Vlahovich N|Pease DL|Shim VB|Besier TF|Zheng M|Cook JL|Pizzolato C
exerciseProtocol: Selected rehabilitation, locomotor, jumping, and landing tasks
externalLoadCondition: Bodyweight locomotion and heel raise with bodyweight or 1.2 bodyweight external-load condition
measurementMethod: Freehand three-dimensional ultrasound with motion analysis
majorLimitations: Small healthy trained sample; strain is not force; conditions are task specific.
sourceNotes: Direct strain observations remain separate from modeled tendon force.

title: Patellar tendon stress between two variations of the forward step lunge.
authors: Zellmer M|Kernozek TW|Gheidi N|Hove J|Torry M
journal: Journal of Sport and Health Science
exerciseProtocol: Forward lunge with knee permitted in front of toes versus maintained behind toes
measurementMethod: Motion analysis and inverse dynamics with tendon stress estimation
measuredOutcome: Estimated patellar tendon stress, stress impulse, and stress rate
majorLimitations: Condition comparison does not establish app-wide bands; abstract does not expose every numeric table value.
sourceNotes: Used to preserve metric separation and lunge-condition specificity, not to manufacture thresholds.

title: Measurement of in vivo anterior cruciate ligament strain during dynamic jump landing.
authors: Taylor KA|Terry ME|Utturkar GM|Spritzer CE|Queen RM|Irribarra LA|Garrett WE|DeFrate LE
exerciseProtocol: Single-leg jump landing under the study protocol
measurementMethod: MRI, motion analysis, and biplanar fluoroscopy
majorLimitations: Small sample; peak occurred before ground impact; protocol-specific strain is not a universal landing value.
sourceNotes: Timing and dispersion are preserved with the extracted measurement.

title: Preventive lateral ligament tester (PLLT): a novel method to evaluate mechanical properties of lateral ankle joint ligaments in the intact ankle.
exerciseProtocol: Preventive lateral ligament tester at three inversion loads
measurementMethod: Instrumented inversion testing with ligament-force estimation
majorLimitations: Passive standardized inversion is not an exercise or landing protocol; healthy and cadaver components must not be generalized to app exercise loads.
sourceNotes: Retained as boundary evidence only; no app claim or rubric is created.

title: Biomechanical analysis of hip, knee, and ankle joint contact forces during squats in elite powerlifters.
authors: Pürzel A|Kaufmann P|Koller W|Wallnöfer EK|Baca A|Kainz H
measurementMethod: Three-dimensional motion capture, force plates, and musculoskeletal modeling
measuredOutcome: Modeled hip, tibiofemoral, patellofemoral, and ankle joint contact force
reportedMetric: Peak joint contact force at 90 percent 1RM
majorLimitations: Elite population; high relative loads; model-specific joint contact estimates cannot be merged with bodyweight-task models.
sourceNotes: Creates source-specific high-load candidates without a cross-model rubric.
